title: Agentic Legal Platform — System Design
description: Design the platform that lets legal agents do real work — contract review and redlining, due-diligence at scale, and cited research memos — where the irreversible action behind the gate is a finalized or filed work product, with malpractice and privilege on the line. It builds on Agentic Platform Foundations (retrieval/grounding, eval, confidentiality) and, like the Finance vertical, leans deterministic: retrieve from authoritative sources → draft → verify every citation → attorney review. Its crux is a citation-verification gate (existence, currency, and relevance — a real-but-overruled citation is worse than a fabricated one) plus a mandatory attorney-in-the-loop, because hallucination is a uniquely dangerous, first-class risk.

nodes.label: Supervising Attorney
nodes.description: Licensed attorney who must review and approve before any work product is finalized or filed.

nodes.label: Matter Documents
nodes.description: Client matter inputs: contracts, due-diligence sets, the drafting request.

nodes.label: Legal API
nodes.description: Authenticates the matter, enforces tenant isolation, routes to the pipeline.

nodes.label: Research / Draft Pipeline
nodes.description: Deterministic DAG: retrieve -> draft -> verify -> attorney review. Not an open loop.

nodes.label: Draft Agent
nodes.description: LLM inside a bounded node: drafts memos/redlines grounded in retrieved sources, with citations.

nodes.label: Inference Backend
nodes.description: Shared LLM serving tier (from the Foundations platform), under no-train terms.

nodes.label: Authoritative Source Index
nodes.description: Grounding corpus: Westlaw / Practical Law / KeyCite and statutes — the citable, authoritative law.

nodes.label: Document Mgmt System
nodes.description: iManage/NetDocuments matter docs, preserving access controls and ethical walls.

nodes.label: Citation Verifier
nodes.description: Deterministic check of every citation: existence, currency (not overruled, via KeyCite), and relevance to the proposition.

nodes.label: Draft Work Product
nodes.description: Pending drafts with citations and provenance, awaiting attorney approval — never auto-filed.

nodes.label: Provenance Store
nodes.description: Maps every finding/sentence to the source span it came from (audit-defensible).

nodes.label: Confidentiality & Policy
nodes.description: Tenant isolation, ethical walls, zero-data-retention/no-train enforcement, document screening.

nodes.label: Identity & Token Broker
nodes.description: Scoped, delegated access to the matter; ties approval to a licensed attorney principal.

nodes.label: Audit Record
nodes.description: Append-only record: sources retrieved + versions, citations verified, attorney approval, event IDs.

nodes.label: Work Queue
nodes.description: Levels intake and carries due-diligence batches (tabular extract over many docs).

nodes.label: Observability & Eval
nodes.description: Trajectories, citation-error and hallucination rates, eval vs attorney ground truth.

functional: Review contracts against a playbook and produce redlines with rationale.
Run due-diligence review at scale (tabular extraction across thousands of documents).
Draft research memos and documents grounded in authoritative legal sources, with citations.
Verify every citation before any draft is surfaced to an attorney.
Require attorney review and approval before any work product is finalized or filed.
nonFunctional: Hallucination is a first-class risk: a wrong, overruled, or off-point citation must be caught, not surfaced.
Every finding is traceable to a source span (audit-defensible provenance).
Confidentiality and isolation: zero-data-retention, no-train, tenant segregation, ethical walls, SOC 2.
Grounding is over authoritative law (Westlaw/Practical Law/KeyCite) and matter docs, not the open web.
Deterministic control flow: retrieve → draft → verify → review, not an open autonomous loop.

capacity.label: Citation verification
capacity.note: No draft reaches an attorney until existence, currency, and relevance are checked.

capacity.label: Autonomy
capacity.note: Agents produce pending drafts; a licensed attorney finalizes — nothing auto-files.

capacity.label: Due-diligence scale
capacity.note: Tabular extraction fans across large document sets, each finding traced to its source span.

api.description: The pipeline produces a draft work product (grounded, cited, provenance-tracked) for attorney review — it does not file.
A licensed attorney reviews and approves; only then is the work product finalized.
Fetch the provenance map: each finding/sentence linked to the source span it came from, plus citation-verification results.

dataModel.name: drafts
dataModel.note: A pending work product awaiting attorney approval — never auto-filed.

dataModel.name: citations
dataModel.note: Each citation in a draft and its verification status.

dataModel.name: provenance
dataModel.note: Maps a finding to the exact source span it came from.

patterns.name: Workflow vs agent (deterministic pipeline vs probabilistic loop)
patterns.what: An authored DAG (retrieve -> draft -> verify -> review), not an open autonomous loop.
patterns.whenToUse: When a wrong citation is a malpractice-grade side effect to guard before surfacing.

patterns.name: Human-in-the-loop approval (gate)
patterns.what: A licensed attorney reviews and approves before any work product is finalized or filed.
patterns.whenToUse: Before any legal work product leaves the platform.

patterns.name: Bounded autonomy (agent + deterministic gate)
patterns.what: Agents draft freely but a deterministic verify gate and attorney review fence the irreversible action.
patterns.whenToUse: Draft-only autonomy with hard verification and review boundaries.

patterns.name: LLM-as-judge / trajectory evaluation
patterns.what: Verify citations and evaluate drafts against attorney ground truth; track hallucination and citation-error rates.
patterns.whenToUse: Measuring and gating the pipeline where hallucination is the core risk.

patterns.name: Memory: short-term vs long-term (semantic/episodic/procedural)
patterns.what: Retrieve from authoritative law and matter docs as grounding, scoped per tenant.
patterns.whenToUse: Grounding every draft in citable sources, not model memory.

patterns.name: Prompt-injection defense (CaMeL / Dual-LLM / Plan-then-Execute)
patterns.what: Treat matter documents as untrusted; partition them from tool authority and from other tenants.
patterns.whenToUse: Documents and opposing-party text are attacker-reachable.

patterns.name: Delegation not impersonation (OAuth token exchange, act claim)
patterns.what: Scoped, delegated matter access; approval is tied to a licensed attorney principal.
patterns.whenToUse: Carrying matter authority and binding approval to a licensed user.

patterns.name: Queue-based load leveling; admission control
patterns.what: Level intake and fan due-diligence batches across thousands of documents.

steps.title: 1. Naive: An Agent That Drafts From Its Own Knowledge (the baseline)
description: Start with the tempting demo: an agent answers a legal question or drafts a memo straight from the model, citing cases from memory.

In law this is the malpractice version. Models hallucinate citations — and worse than a fabricated case is a real but overruled one that reads as good law. There's no grounding in authoritative sources, no citation check, no provenance, no attorney review, and no confidentiality story for client data sent to a model that might train on it.
The rest of the interview turns this into a grounded, verified, attorney-gated pipeline.

view.caption: An agent drafts from model memory and cites from memory — the hallucinated-citation malpractice baseline.

steps.decisionPrompt: Why is a confidently cited but unverified draft more dangerous in law than in most domains?
patterns: Workflow vs agent (deterministic pipeline vs probabilistic loop)
whyNow: The naive draft makes the hallucination and verification requirements concrete.

recap.after: An agent can produce a plausible legal draft.
recap.newRisk: Citations may be fabricated or overruled, nothing is grounded or verified, and client data isn't protected.

traps.trap: Trusting a fluent draft because its citations look real.
traps.why: A real-but-overruled citation reads as good law; fluency is not correctness, and the model can't tell.
traps.instead: Ground in authoritative sources and verify every citation before an attorney sees it.

steps.title: 2. Ground in Authoritative Law, Not Model Memory
description: The grounding corpus for this vertical is authoritative legal sources — Westlaw / Practical Law / KeyCite and statutes — plus the client's matter documents in the DMS. The agent must draft from retrieved, citable sources, not from the model's recollection.
Retrieval is scoped per tenant and per matter, and matter-document access preserves the firm's ethical walls.

view.caption: The agent grounds drafts in retrieved authoritative law and matter docs, scoped per tenant with ethical walls preserved.

steps.decisionPrompt: Why ground in Westlaw/KeyCite and the DMS rather than the open web or the model's own knowledge?

concepts.definition: Retrieving from curated legal databases and matter docs so every assertion can cite a real, current source.
concepts.whyItMatters: It is the only way to make citations checkable; ungrounded drafting is unverifiable by construction.
concepts.example: A proposition is drafted from a retrieved Westlaw headnote, with the case and its KeyCite status attached.

whyNow: Grounding is the corpus that makes verification possible; everything later assumes citable sources.

recap.before: The agent drafted from model memory.
recap.after: Drafts are grounded in retrieved authoritative law and matter docs.
recap.newRisk: Grounding alone doesn't guarantee the cited source is current or on-point — it must be verified.

steps.title: 3. A Deterministic Retrieve → Draft → Verify Pipeline
description: Because a wrong citation is a malpractice-grade side effect that must be guarded before the work product is surfaced, the control plane is a deterministic pipeline — retrieve → draft → verify → attorney review — with LLM calls confined to bounded nodes, not an open autonomous loop (the §2 selection rule).
The draft node grounds and cites; the verify node (next step) checks; the pipeline writes a pending draft. Due-diligence work fans across thousands of documents through the same authored path.

view.caption: An authored DAG: the model drafts in a bounded node grounded in authoritative law; the pipeline writes a pending draft.

steps.decisionPrompt: Why a fixed pipeline rather than an autonomous research agent that decides its own steps?
patterns: Workflow vs agent (deterministic pipeline vs probabilistic loop)

concepts.definition: Every citation is checked before a draft is ever shown to an attorney, not after.
concepts.whyItMatters: It guards the malpractice-grade side effect at the right point — before the human invests trust in the draft.
concepts.example: A draft with an overruled citation is flagged and corrected before it reaches the review queue.

whyNow: A known, auditable path lets verification sit as a mandatory stage before review.

options.name: Deterministic pipeline (recommended)
pros: Verification is a mandatory, fixed stage.
Predictable and auditable; provenance at each step.
cons: Less flexible for novel research paths.

options.name: Autonomous research loop
cons: Can skip or defer verification; non-deterministic and hard to audit.
Wrong default when a bad citation is malpractice.

recap.before: Drafts could come from an opaque research loop.
recap.after: A deterministic pipeline produces each grounded draft.
recap.newRisk: The draft's citations still must be proven current and on-point before anyone relies on them.

steps.title: 4. The Gate: Verify Every Citation (Existence, Currency, Relevance)
description: This is the crux. A deterministic citation verifier checks every citation on three axes before a draft is surfaced: existence (the source is real), currency (it is still good law — KeyCite flags overruled or questioned authority), and relevance (it actually supports the proposition it's attached to).

Currency is what makes this domain special: a real but overruled citation is worse than a fabricated one, because it reads as authoritative. Any flagged citation sends the draft back for correction, not forward to the attorney.

view.caption: Every citation is checked for existence, currency (KeyCite), and relevance; a flag returns the draft for correction.

steps.decisionPrompt: Why is a real-but-overruled citation a worse failure than a citation that simply doesn't exist?

concepts.definition: The three checks every citation must pass: it is real, it is still good law, and it supports the stated proposition.
concepts.whyItMatters: Existence alone is not enough — currency catches overruled law and relevance catches off-point cites.
concepts.example: A case exists and is on-point but KeyCite shows it was overruled -> flagged, draft returned.

whyNow: Verification is the first-class component this vertical is built around; it precedes any human review.

recap.after: Every citation is proven real, current, and on-point before review.
recap.newRisk: Even a fully verified draft is not work product until a licensed attorney approves it.

traps.trap: Checking only that a cited case exists.
traps.why: An existing case can be overruled or off-point; existence is the weakest of the three checks.
traps.instead: Verify currency (KeyCite) and relevance too, and return the draft on any flag.

steps.title: 5. The Gate, Part Two: Attorney-in-the-Loop
description: Verification makes a draft trustworthy; it does not make it work product. A licensed attorney must review and approve before anything is finalized or filed — this is mandatory, not optional, and the platform binds approval to a licensed attorney principal via identity.
The agent and pipeline are the assistant; the supervising attorney remains responsible (UPL and the professional-conduct rules on supervising non-lawyer/AI assistance). Nothing auto-files.

view.caption: A licensed attorney reviews the verified draft; identity binds approval to the attorney before the work product is finalized.

steps.decisionPrompt: What does the platform check to ensure the approver is a licensed attorney and not the agent itself?
patterns: Delegation not impersonation (OAuth token exchange, act claim)

concepts.definition: A licensed attorney must review and approve AI-assisted work before it becomes finalized or filed work product.
concepts.whyItMatters: Responsibility and the unauthorized-practice line stay with the attorney; the agent cannot be the final authority.
concepts.example: The draft is marked finalized only after the identity broker confirms an attorney principal approved it.

whyNow: The human gate is what makes draft-only autonomy safe for an irreversible, professionally-regulated output.

recap.before: A verified draft could be treated as finished.
recap.after: Only attorney-approved drafts become finalized work product.
recap.newRisk: Client data and findings must be confidential and every finding defensible to a court.

steps.title: 6. Confidentiality & Audit-Defensible Provenance
description: Two non-negotiables sit under everything. Confidentiality: client data runs under zero-data-retention and no-train terms, with tenant segregation and ethical walls enforced — privilege is genuinely at risk otherwise, and matter documents are untrusted input that must be partitioned from other tenants and from tool authority.

Provenance: every finding and sentence traces to the exact source span it came from, so the work is audit-defensible — an attorney (or a court) can follow any assertion back to its source and version.

view.caption: Confidentiality (ZDR/no-train, tenant isolation, ethical walls) plus provenance: every finding traces to a source span.

steps.decisionPrompt: How do you make every sentence in a memo defensible back to a source, and keep one client's data away from another's?

concepts.definition: A mapping from each finding to the exact source document, span, and version it was drawn from.
concepts.whyItMatters: Legal work must be defensible; a finding without a traceable source can't be relied on or defended.
concepts.example: A diligence finding links to 'Contract v2, §7.3, lines 4-9'.

whyNow: Confidentiality and provenance are hard requirements that the gate and audit record depend on.

deepDives.title: Why hallucination is treated as first-class here
points: A dated Stanford study reported material hallucination even on grounded legal tools (tied to the model versions tested — not a timeless benchmark).
So verification and provenance are components, not afterthoughts.
Keep raw model traces in a separate, access-controlled store under the same ZDR/no-train terms.

recap.before: Client data and findings had no isolation or traceability.
recap.after: ZDR/no-train confidentiality with tenant isolation, and every finding traced to a source span.
recap.newRisk: We still need to support the real workflows and measure quality where hallucination is the core failure.

steps.title: 7. Workflows at Scale & Evaluation
description: Support the real legal workflows on this substrate: contract review against a playbook with redline rationale; due-diligence review at scale via tabular extraction across thousands of documents (each cell traced to a source span); and cited research memos.

Evaluate where it matters: against attorney ground truth, tracking citation-error and hallucination rates with trajectory eval, so a fluent-looking output never hides a bad-law citation or an unsupported finding.

view.caption: Contract review, due-diligence tabular extract, and cited memos run on the pipeline; quality is evaluated vs attorney ground truth.

steps.decisionPrompt: A memo reads beautifully but cites one overruled case — which signal catches it, fluency or citation-error eval?

concepts.definition: Measuring how often the system produces non-existent, overruled, off-point, or unsupported citations, against attorney-verified ground truth.
concepts.whyItMatters: It is the domain's defining quality metric; final-answer fluency hides exactly the failure that matters.
concepts.example: Track the rate of flagged citations per memo and unsupported findings per diligence set.

whyNow: Workflows and eval are what let the platform deliver real legal work without losing defensibility.

recap.before: The platform handled one task and quality was unmeasured.
recap.after: Contract review, diligence, and memos run on one verified, attorney-gated pipeline, evaluated against ground truth.
recap.newRisk: This completes phase 1 of the series — Foundations plus three verticals contrasting where the gate sits.

finalDesign.title: Final Design — Agentic Legal Platform
finalDesign.description: Matter documents and a request enter through the legal API and queue into a deterministic pipeline. The draft agent grounds its work in retrieved authoritative law (Westlaw/Practical Law/KeyCite) and matter docs from the DMS, drafting with citations in a bounded LLM node. A deterministic citation verifier checks every citation for existence, currency (KeyCite), and relevance — any flag returns the draft for correction. The pipeline records provenance for every finding (source span and version) and writes a pending draft. A licensed attorney reviews and approves; the identity broker binds finalization to an attorney principal, and nothing auto-files. Confidentiality runs throughout (zero-data-retention, no-train, tenant isolation, ethical walls), every action appends to an immutable audit record, and the pipeline is evaluated against attorney ground truth on citation-error and hallucination rates. Retrieval/grounding, eval, and confidentiality are the shared substrate from Agentic Platform Foundations; like the Finance vertical the platform leans deterministic, but the irreversible action it guards is a finalized or filed work product.

functional.how: Playbook-grounded drafting on the deterministic pipeline.
Tabular extraction fanned across documents, each finding traced.
Grounded drafting in authoritative law with citations.
Mandatory attorney-in-the-loop bound to a licensed principal.

nonFunctional.how: Citation verification is a mandatory pipeline stage.
Every finding maps to a source span and version.
Retrieval over Westlaw/Practical Law/KeyCite and matter docs.
Retrieve -> draft -> verify -> review, not a loop.

say: Frame the irreversible action as a finalized/filed work product, with malpractice and privilege at stake.
It builds on Agentic Platform Foundations; like Finance it leans deterministic.
Draw the agent drafting from memory and explain why a real-but-overruled citation is the worst failure.
Ground in authoritative law and the DMS, then a deterministic retrieve -> draft -> verify pipeline.
The citation-verification gate (existence/currency/relevance), the mandatory attorney-in-the-loop, then confidentiality and provenance.
Contract review, due-diligence tabular extract, memos, and eval against attorney ground truth on citation errors.

expectations: Agent drafts grounded in retrieved sources.
Authoritative grounding and a deterministic verify pipeline.
Treats currency (overruled law) as the key citation check, not just existence.
Designs audit-defensible provenance and ZDR/no-train confidentiality.
Picks deterministic control flow via the selection rule; evaluates on citation-error rate; builds on the Foundations substrate.

followUps: How do you verify citation currency at scale without a human checking every cite?
How do you keep one client's matter data isolated from another's through retrieval and the model?
What goes in the audit-defensible provenance record for a due-diligence finding?
How does this work-product gate compare to the finance vertical's money-movement gate?
